extracted_json.title: Business Goals and Strategies of Tech Companies

blocks.text: Example: Microsoft's mission statement (≈ 1985): "Every one of these computers will be controlled (or managed) using a MS Operating System (OS)"

items.text: Growth (% per year) in Sales Revenue
Profit Margin (%) = Net Income x 100 % / Sales Rev.

blocks.text: Every tech. company has (or needs to articulate) three important strategies:

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Business goals and the three core strategies of a technology company.
extracted_json.summary_gist: Notes give Microsoft's ~1985 mission statement as an example, then list business goals (BGs) including annual sales revenue, growth, profit/net income, and profit margin (Net Income / Sales Rev. × 100%). It concludes that every tech company has three key strategies: competitive, technology (TS), and product/market (P/M s).

transcription:
Example :   Microsoft's mission statement (≈ 1985):
            "Every one of these computers
             will be controlled (or managed)
             using a  MS Operating System (OS)"

1.3   Business Goals (BGs)
        - Annual Sales Revenue ($)
        - Growth (% per year) in Sales Revenue
        - Profit ($) or Net Income ($)
        - Profit Margin (%) = Net Income x 100 %
                              ──────────
                              Sales Rev.

        - . . . .
        - . . . .
_______________________________________

      Every tech. company has (or needs to
articulate) three important strategies :
        - its Competitive strategy (see L #3, 4)
        - Technology strategy (TS)

        - Product / Market strategy (P/M s)
